skill.name: fullstack-dev
skill.description: Full-stack backend architecture and frontend-backend integration guide for building complete web applications with REST APIs, authentication, and real-time features.
skill.summary: Comprehensive full-stack development guide covering backend architecture, API design, auth flows, database patterns, and production deployment.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Scanned 9 files (3841 lines). Static analyzer flagged 721 potential issues but most are false positives. All flagged patterns are in markdown documentation showing legitimate code examples for build/test commands, auth flows, and config patterns. No malicious intent found. Skill is safe for publication with standard warnings.

high_findings.title: High Volume of Pattern Matches in Documentation
high_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 721 potential security issues across the skill files. Upon evaluation, the majority of these are false positives triggered by documentation patterns (markdown code blocks showing shell commands, API examples, and config patterns). The skill contains no executable malicious code - all flagged patterns appear in markdown documentation context.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged patterns are in markdown code blocks showing legitimate development examples (build commands, curl tests, API patterns). No actual malicious code execution patterns exist.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Documentation
medium_findings.description: Skill contains documentation for running build/test commands (npm run build, curl tests). These are example commands for the MANDATORY WORKFLOW section, not actual command execution. The skill is a guide/reference document, not executable code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines show build commands (cd server && npm run build) which are documentation examples in Step 4 verification section, not actual executing code.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Documentation
medium_findings.description: Skill references environment variable patterns (process.env.DB_HOST, os.environ) for configuration best practices. This is educational content about how to properly configure applications, not credential exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable references are in documentation context showing proper configuration patterns, not dynamic access of secrets for exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Network Request Patterns in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Skill includes examples of API calls (fetch, axios patterns) and WebSocket/SSE implementations for real-time features. These are legitimate code examples for building full-stack applications.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network patterns are for legitimate API client implementations with auth flows. No suspicious external endpoints or data exfiltration patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: References to localhost URLs and example endpoints (http://localhost:3000/health) in documentation. These are standard development examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are localhost development examples in Step 4 smoke test section. Standard practice for documentation.

low_findings.title: Cryptography Pattern References
low_findings.description: Skill references cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1, bcrypt, etc.) in the context of security best practices and what NOT to use. Educational content, not actual insecure implementations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Crypto references are in documentation about best practices (e.g., 'do not use MD5 for passwords'). No actual weak crypto implementations in the skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Keywords in Context
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged terms like 'scan', 'probe', 'discover' in the context of database schema design and testing strategies. These are standard software engineering terms, not reconnaissance tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Keywords appear in context of 'schema scanning for migrations' and 'test discovery' - standard development terminology.

dangerous_patterns.title: Heuristic: Code Execution + Network + Credential Access Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged this combination pattern across multiple files. EVALUATION: This is a false positive. The skill is a development guide that documents: (1) build/test commands for verification workflows, (2) API client implementations with auth token handling, (3) environment configuration patterns. All are legitimate development practices documented in markdown, not actual malicious code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combination occurs in documentation showing proper development patterns - build verification commands, API auth flows, and config management. No malicious behavior intent detected.

content.value_statement: Building full-stack applications requires coordinating backend APIs, authentication, databases, and frontend integration. This skill provides a structured workflow and best practices for creating robust, production-ready applications with proper error handling and testing.
actual_capabilities: Design and implement REST APIs with proper versioning, pagination, and error handling
Implement JWT-based authentication flows with token refresh and secure cookie handling
Create service layer architecture with dependency injection and error boundaries
Configure environment management with .env files and secrets rotation
Set up real-time features using Server-Sent Events (SSE) or WebSocket
Generate database schemas with migrations and proper indexing strategies
limitations: Does not include UI/UX design guidance or CSS/styling recommendations
Does not provide hosting infrastructure setup or CI/CD pipeline configuration
Does not cover specific framework installation commands or dependency troubleshooting
Requires manual code implementation - does not generate complete working applications

use_cases.title: Scaffolding a new full-stack application
use_cases.description: When starting a new project, use the mandatory workflow to define stack, architecture, and auth strategy before writing any code. This ensures all components work together from the start.

use_cases.title: Adding authentication to an existing API
use_cases.description: Integrate JWT bearer flow with automatic token refresh into existing Express or Django APIs. Follow the auth middleware ordering patterns to ensure proper protection.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers adding security to existing services

use_cases.title: Implementing real-time updates
use_cases.description: Add Server-Sent Events or WebSocket support to existing applications for live notifications, collaborative features, or dashboard updates without polling overhead.
use_cases.target_user: Developers adding real-time capabilities to existing apps

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold new full-stack project
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a new full-stack application using {backend_stack} and {frontend_stack}. I need REST API endpoints for {resource_name} with CRUD operations, JWT authentication, and proper error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new full-stack application from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Add authentication to API
prompt_templates.prompt: Add JWT authentication to my existing {framework} API. Include login/logout endpoints, access token (15min) and refresh token (7d httpOnly cookie) flow, and middleware to protect existing routes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding security to an existing API

prompt_templates.title: Implement real-time notifications
prompt_templates.prompt: Add real-time notifications to my application using {sse_or_websocket}. Users should receive updates when {event_type} happens without page refresh.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding live updates to existing application

prompt_templates.title: Design database schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a database schema for {domain} with {table_count} tables. Include proper indexes for common queries, foreign key relationships, and migration scripts for {db_type}.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating database structure for new feature

output_examples.input: Build a todo API with Express and React frontend
output_examples.output: Created full-stack todo application with Express backend (JWT auth, PostgreSQL) and React frontend. Backend includes /api/todos CRUD endpoints, /api/auth/login with refresh token flow, and proper error handling. Frontend includes TodoApp component with API client wrapper that handles 401 automatic refresh.

output_examples.input: Add SSE real-time updates to existing Node.js app
output_examples.output: Implemented Server-Sent Events in existing Express app. Created /api/events endpoint with connection management, added client-side EventSource integration, and handled reconnection with exponential backoff. Frontend displays live updates without manual refresh.

best_practices: Always define architectural decisions (stack, API style, auth strategy) before writing code - document these in a decisions.json file
Implement error handling with typed error hierarchies - never expose raw error messages to clients in production
Use the mandatory verification workflow - run build checks, smoke tests, and integration tests before claiming completion
anti_patterns: Do not skip authentication middleware ordering - always validate JWT before checking permissions, not after
Avoid hardcoding URLs or credentials in code - use environment variables with validation at startup
Do not implement real-time features with polling when SSE or WebSocket is more appropriate - polling wastes resources

faq.question: What stack does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports any backend (Express/Node.js, Django/Python, Go) and frontend (React, Vue, Next.js) combination. The principles apply universally though examples may show specific frameworks.

faq.question: Does this skill include hosting or deployment?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses on development patterns and code structure. For deployment, consider dedicated DevOps skills for Docker, Kubernetes, or specific cloud platforms.

faq.question: How does authentication work across frontend and backend?
faq.answer: The skill documents JWT bearer flow with short-lived access tokens (15 minutes) and long-lived refresh tokens (7 days) stored in httpOnly cookies. The frontend API client handles automatic token refresh on 401 responses.

faq.question: When should I use SSE vs WebSocket?
faq.answer: Use SSE for one-way server-to-client updates (notifications, live data). Use WebSocket for bidirectional communication (chat, collaborative editing, gaming). SSE is simpler and works over HTTP/2 automatically.

faq.question: Does this skill cover database-specific optimization?
faq.answer: The skill covers general database patterns (schema design, migrations, indexing) but does not include database-specific tuning. For performance optimization, consult database-specific documentation after basic setup.

faq.question: How do I test the backend and frontend together?
faq.answer: Use the Step 4 verification workflow: run backend build (npm run build in /server), start the server, test endpoints with curl, then verify frontend can connect via CORS and API base URL configuration.
